tldr: Bloomreach is a hosted commerce search/personalization platform; fd is an OSS CLI file finder. These are not feature-equivalent, so a direct migration is not realistic. If you only need filesystem search, fd works; for product/site search you need a different replacement.
why_switch: Eliminate subscription costs by moving to free OSS tooling
Remove vendor lock-in tied to a hosted commerce search platform
Simpler stack if your real need was local/file-based search, not product search
what_to_export: Search index schema and field mappings
API integration code (SDK calls, frontend widgets)
Access roles and API keys inventory
export_method: Use Bloomreach admin/console export tools and APIs to download catalog feeds, index configs, and rules. Confirm exact endpoints in current Bloomreach docs before relying on any specific path.
import_method: fd has no index or catalog concept — it scans a filesystem path on demand. There is nothing to import; install fd (cargo, brew, apt, etc.) and run queries against directories. For real product search you would import the exported feed into a different engine (e.g. self-hosted search), not fd.

concept_mapping.note: fd has no ranking control beyond match order

effort: 1-3 days if scope is truly filesystem search; weeks-to-months if you need to replace commerce search functionality with another platform
gotchas: fd is not a SaaS search replacement — most Bloomreach features have no fd equivalent
No relevance tuning, no analytics, no merchandising in fd
Frontend search widgets and storefront integrations must be rebuilt or replaced
Re-evaluate whether the pairing makes sense before committing to migration

email.subject: Cancelling Bloomreach subscription
email.body:
Hi team,

We are winding down our use of Bloomreach and will not be renewing. Please confirm the cancellation effective date, any data export options for our catalog and configuration, and the data retention/deletion policy after cancellation.

Kindly share final invoices and confirm closure of our account and API credentials.

Thanks.
